title: Metal working machine operatives
description: Create or reproduce handmade objects for sale and exhibition using a variety of techniques, such as welding, weaving, pottery, and needlecraft. Construct, assemble, maintain, and repair stationary steam boilers and boiler house auxiliaries. Align structures or plate sections to assemble boiler frame tanks or vats, following blueprints. Work involves use of hand and power tools, plumb bobs, levels, wedges, dogs, or turnbuckles. Assist in testing assembled vessels. Direct cleaning of boilers and boiler furnaces. Inspect and repair boiler fittings, such as safety valves, regulators, automatic-control mechanisms, water columns, and auxiliary machines. Fabricate, assemble, install, and repair sheet metal products and equipment, such as ducts, control boxes, drainpipes, and furnace casings. Work may involve any of the following: setting up and operating fabricating machines to cut, bend, and straighten sheet metal; shaping metal over anvils, blocks, or forms using hammer; operating soldering and welding equipment to join sheet metal parts; or inspecting, assembling, and smoothing seams and joints of burred surfaces. Includes sheet metal duct installers who install prefabricated sheet metal ducts used for heating, air conditioning, or other purposes. (Based on 51 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Inspect, test, or troubleshoot malfunctioning equipment, following specifications and using test and analysis instruments.
Disassemble malfunctioning equipment to gain access to defects, using hand tools for inspection, repair, or replacement of parts.
Repair or replace broken, damaged, or worn parts on various equipment, using hand tools, power tools, and precision measuring instruments.
Fabricate, assemble, install, and repair components and systems, following blueprints, diagrams, and technical manuals.
Perform preventive maintenance on equipment, including cleaning, lubricating, and adjusting parts to ensure proper operation.
Test and calibrate equipment performance, focusing on specific parameters and using precision gauges and measuring instruments.
Monitor machine operations and make necessary adjustments, stopping machines to address issues as they arise.
Measure parts and verify dimensions to ensure compliance with specifications, using precision measuring tools.
Document maintenance and repair activities, including inspection results, work performed, and parts requisitions.
Set up and operate grinding or polishing machines to grind metal workpieces, ensuring quality and precision in the final product.
Select appropriate tools, safety equipment, and parts according to job requirements.
Use hand tools and power tools such as screwdrivers, pliers, wrenches, and welding equipment.
Inspect, repair, and maintain automotive and mechanical equipment and machinery, including pumps and compressors.
Perform routine maintenance such as changing oil, lubricating equipment, and checking batteries.
Inspect and verify dimensions and clearances of parts for conformity to factory specifications.
Dismount, mount, and repair or replace tires, and check condition or pressure of tires.
Repair or replace parts such as pistons, gears, valves, and bearings.
Test drive vehicles to diagnose malfunctions or ensure proper operation, checking brakes and steering.
Inspect repaired vehicles and test components to ensure proper functioning and alignment.
Maintain cleanliness of work area and tidy up the shop after completing tasks.
Estimate costs of vehicle repair and compute costs of labor and materials for production.
Shape and straighten damaged or twisted articles using hand tools and check for wear.
Create jewelry from materials such as gold, silver, platinum, and precious or semiprecious stones.
Smooth soldered joints and rough spots, using hand files and emery paper, and polish smoothed areas with polishing wheels or buffing wire.
Dispose of hazardous waste in an appropriate manner.
Remove grease, dirt, paint, or rust from surfaces in preparation for paint application, using abrasives, solvents, or brushes.
Examine completed objects to determine thicknesses of metal deposits or measure thicknesses using instruments such as micrometers.
Inspect assembled vessels or individual components, such as tubes, fittings, or valves, to locate any defects.
Repair or replace defective parts, such as safety valves or regulators, using hand tools and welding equipment.
Install manholes, handholes, taps, tubes, valves, gauges, or feedwater connections in drums of water tube boilers, using hand tools.
Lay out, measure, and mark dimensions and reference lines on materials such as roofing panels, using calculators, scribes, or rules.
Repair and maintain equipment, objects, or parts, using hand tools and making adjustments as necessary.
Shape seams, joints, or irregular edges of pressure vessel sections or structural parts, using cutting torches, hammers, or files.
Assemble pattern sections, using tools such as bolts, screws, rivets, glue, or welding equipment.
Repair and rework templates and patterns, and clean and finish them using tools like emery cloths and grinders.
Lay out and draw patterns onto material using compasses, protractors, rulers, or scribes.
Study blueprints, sketches, and specifications to plan sequences of operations for fabricating tools or assemblies.
Verify dimensions and alignments of finished parts using measuring instruments like calipers and micrometers.
Devise and construct tools, dies, and jigs, or modify existing tools and equipment.
Program and operate computer numerical control (CNC) machines to cut, bore, or shape parts.
Inspect and test products to verify compliance with specifications using precision measuring instruments.
Cut, shape, and form metal parts using lathes, power saws, and other machine tools.
Grind, file, and sand parts to finished dimensions, and smooth and polish surfaces of parts or tools.
Dismantle machines and remove parts for repair, using hand tools, hoists, and cranes.
Clean and lubricate machines, parts, and tools, and perform routine maintenance work.
Repair or replace defective parts of machinery or equipment using hand tools and machine tools.
Inspect and test mechanical products after repair or assembly to ensure proper performance.
Direct workers who are assembling or disassembling equipment or cleaning parts.
Level bedplate and establish centerline, using straightedge, levels, and transit.
Dismantle machinery and equipment for maintenance and shipment, using hammers, wrenches, and other hand tools.
Construct foundations for machines, using hand tools and materials such as wood, cement, and steel.
Operate engine lathe to grind, file, and turn machine parts to dimensional specifications using machine tools.
Conduct preventative maintenance, repair, and lubrication of machines and equipment.
Weld, repair, and fabricate equipment or machinery, ensuring conformance to specifications.
Assemble and align frames, gears, and gear systems, ensuring proper fit and operation.
Select, install, and adjust alignment of drills, cutters, dies, guides, and holding devices using measuring instruments and hand tools.
Measure, examine, and test completed units to check for defects and ensure conformance to specifications.
Set up and operate machines to saw, cut, shear, notch, bend, or straighten metal or plastic materials according to specifications.
Read blueprints or job orders to determine product specifications, tooling instructions, and operational sequences for machining operations.
Inspect workpieces for defects, and measure them to determine accuracy of machine operations, using rules or other measuring instruments.
Extract or lift jammed pieces from machines using fingers or hand tools, and remove burrs or sharp edges from workpieces.
Record operational data, such as pressure readings and feed rates, and monitor the feed and speed of machines during operations.
Plan and develop layouts from blueprints and templates, applying knowledge of design and properties of metals.
Lift and position workpieces in relation to machining surfaces, either manually or using hoists.
Inspect sample workpieces to verify conformance with specifications, using instruments such as gauges, micrometers, and dial indicators.
Replace worn tools and sharpen dull cutting tools, using hand tools and bench grinders.
Adjust machine controls and change tool settings to keep dimensions within specified tolerances.
Study blueprints, layouts, sketches, or work orders to assess workpiece specifications and to determine tooling instructions and material needs.
Select and install cutting tools or accessories according to specifications, using hand tools or power tools.
Set up, operate, or tend grinding and related tools that remove excess material or burrs from surfaces or sharpen edges.
Clean work areas and lubricate machines, tools, or parts using solvents or lubricating compounds.
Perform preventative maintenance or minor repairs on machines, and troubleshoot or make adjustments to correct problems.
Lift metal stock or workpieces manually or using hoists, and position them in machines with fasteners and hand tools.
Program computer numerical control machines and enter control instructions to start operation.
Observe machines on trial runs or conduct simulations to ensure that programs and machinery will function properly and produce items that meet specifications.
Revise programs or tapes to eliminate errors, and retest to check that problems have been solved.
Determine the sequence of machine operations, and select the proper cutting tools needed to machine workpieces into the desired shapes.
Analyze job orders, drawings, blueprints, specifications, or design data to calculate dimensions, tool selection, and machine speeds.
Determine reference points or hole locations, and compute dimensions, radii, and curvatures for machining.
Inspect, test, and measure completed work using devices to verify conformance to standards or repair requirements.
Perform skilled repair or maintenance operations using hand or power tools, hydraulic presses, or welding equipment.
Inspect and monitor work areas and provide employee safety training to prevent unsafe conditions or violations.
Requisition materials and supplies, such as tools, equipment, or replacement parts needed for operations.
Confer with personnel, such as management or engineering, to coordinate work activities, resolve grievances, or assess resource needs.
